articles.title: Stephen Miller Is Still Pursuing His Immigration Agenda, but More Quietly
articles.summary: The architect of President Trump’s mass deportation campaign wants “a moratorium on immigration from third world countries until we can heal ourselves as a nation.” The chaos in Minneapolis has not pu

articles.title: Easter Reminds Us That Resurrection Is Everywhere
articles.summary: Marriages, careers, reputations, financial stability and dreams can all die. But that’s not the end of the story.

articles.title: ICE Arrests in Texas Leave Children to Raise Their Siblings After Parents Are Detained
articles.summary: Andrea García and her siblings are carrying on in a home reshaped by fear, loss and new responsibility.

articles.title: Will Hungary's far-right leader Viktor Orban be voted out of power?
articles.summary: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Zsuzsanna Vegh, program officer at the German Marshall Fund of the United States, about what's at stake in the upcoming election in Hungary.

articles.title: US airman rescued after his fighter jet was shot down in Iran
articles.summary: NPR's Tom Bowman reports on the race to rescue a U.S. airman after his fighter jet was shot down.

articles.title: Hollywood studios reach a tentative agreement with writers union
articles.summary: The Writers Guild of America went on strike for months in 2023 in a dispute with Hollywood studios. This year the union announced a new four-year contract after just a few weeks of negotiations.

articles.title: Want to Change? Maybe Stop Trying So Hard.
articles.summary: The self-transformation industry sells control. Real change is messier.

articles.title: A Harrowing Race Against Time to Find a Downed U.S. Airman in Iran
articles.summary: For the Iranians, the Air Force colonel whose fighter jet had been shot down was possible leverage. For the U.S. military, finding him was a moral imperative.

articles.title: Trump Revels in Threats to Commit War Crimes in Iran
articles.summary: The president said he would bomb Iran “back to the Stone Ages.” Until this administration, American leaders had insisted they were trying to follow international law in war.

articles.title: Pope Leo XIV Calls for Peace in First Easter Mass
articles.summary: The pontiff’s Easter remarks follow a Palm Sunday homily in which he said God rejected the prayers of “those who wage war.”

articles.title: These rock-climbing fish can shimmy up a 50-foot waterfall
articles.summary: New research from the Democratic Republic of Congo offers a behavioral and anatomical portrait of a species that can achieve surprising athletic feats.

articles.title: In Lebanon, more than 50 medics have been killed by Israel. Some say they're targeted
articles.summary: Lebanon says at least 54 health workers are among more than 1,400 people killed by Israel during the current invasion. Human rights groups say first responders are being targeted — something Israel de

articles.title: What Were Bob Dylan and John Lennon Really Saying in the Back of That Limo?
articles.summary: A film scene of Dylan and Lennon at a low moment in 1966 has held a grim fascination for fans. Beneath the repartee, a touchy issue was at stake.

articles.title: Questions to help you get 'financially naked' with your partner
articles.summary: Having "brutally honest conversations" about money can bring couples closer together, says Vivian Tu, a financial educator. She shares questions to ask your partner at every relationship stage.

articles.title: More teens are getting hooked on gambling. Parents say it often goes undetected
articles.summary: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.

articles.title: Trump unleashes curse-filled social media rant at Iran after U.S. rescues colonel
articles.summary: In a profanity-laden post on Truth Social, President Trump lashed out at Iran and injected new volatility into the conflict, hours after U.S. forces carried out a high-risk rescue mission.

articles.title: Local culture shapes the Zairean liturgy
articles.summary: Easter in the Democratic Republic of the Congo—where congregants celebrate with the Zairean Rite, the only Vatican-approved liturgy shaped by local culture, alive with song, dance, and striking ritual

articles.title: German researchers set right the story of a 9,000-year-old shaman's grave
articles.summary: When a 9,000 year-old grave of a shaman was discovered in Nazi Germany, the discovery was quickly politicized to support Nazi propaganda. But new analysis shows that initial narrative was all wrong.

articles.title: The significance of the space mission that's making history
articles.summary: Former NASA chief of staff Bale Dalton talks about the work that went into the Artemis mission plan and what to watch for on the journey.

articles.title: U.S. searches for airman shot down in Iran, while Trump posts ultimatum
articles.summary: At the start of the sixth week of war, U.S. forces search for a missing airman and President Trump reiterates his ultimatum for Iran to open the Straight of Hormuz.

articles.title: Judge halts Trump effort requiring colleges to show they don't consider race in admissions
articles.summary: A federal judge on Saturday said the Trump Administration the demand to collect data from universities was rolled out in a "rushed and chaotic" manner.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
